description:
You are acting as an on-call monitoring specialist.  
    A sample application log has already been copied to

        /home/user/monitoring/logs/service.log

    Your objective is to prepare two artefacts that another team
    (and our automated checker) will ingest:

    1.  A per-hour count of the number of ERROR lines.
        • Create a file named
              /home/user/monitoring/alerts/error_summary.csv  
        • The file must contain exactly four lines:
              – a header line  
              – one line per hour that contains at least one ERROR  
        • Format rules that **must be followed verbatim**  
              a. Comma-separated, no spaces anywhere in the line.  
              b. Header must be the literal text

                        hour,occurrences

              c. Each data line must contain the two-digit hour
                 (24-hour clock, without leading zero suppression;
                 i.e. “09” would be “9”) and the total number of
                 ERROR lines for that hour.  
              d. Hours must be in ascending numerical order.  
              e. Terminate the file with a single trailing newline.

        Example pattern (DO **NOT** copy these values,
        they are only illustrative):
            hour,occurrences
            3,1
            4,2
            11,5

    2.  A log “snapshot” of the five most recent ERROR entries.
        • Create a file named
              /home/user/monitoring/alerts/latest_errors.log  
        • Copy the last five (5) lines in
              /home/user/monitoring/logs/service.log
          that contain the string “ERROR”, **preserving their
          original ordering and spacing exactly as in the source
          file**.  
        • Append a single newline to the very end of the file.

    Restrictions / hints  
        • You do NOT need root or sudo.  
        • All required information is in the log file given above;
          the current system time is irrelevant.  
        • A standard toolchain (grep, awk, tail, sort, uniq, etc.)
          is available.  The whole job can be done with three-to-four
          short shell commands.  
        • Place the output files in the exact directories given.
          The directories already exist; you need not create them.

    Once both files are in place and meet the formatting
    requirements, the task is complete.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth data for automated grading.
    These values MUST NOT be exposed to the agent.
    -->
    <pre_existing_files>
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/logs/service.log"><![CDATA[
2023-11-01 13:59:59 INFO Service starting
2023-11-01 14:01:12 ERROR Connection lost to DB
2023-11-01 14:05:00 INFO Attempting reconnect
2023-11-01 14:06:47 ERROR Connection lost to DB
2023-11-01 14:50:30 INFO Reconnected to DB
2023-11-01 15:02:03 ERROR Timeout while reading from socket
2023-11-01 15:05:17 WARN High memory usage detected
2023-11-01 15:12:29 ERROR Timeout while reading from socket
2023-11-01 15:30:45 ERROR Timeout while reading from socket
2023-11-01 15:45:00 INFO Memory usage back to normal
2023-11-01 16:00:01 INFO Scheduled report generated
2023-11-01 16:01:02 ERROR Failed to send notification
2023-11-01 16:05:18 ERROR Failed to send notification
2023-11-01 16:10:59 ERROR Failed to send notification
2023-11-01 16:30:00 INFO Service health check passed
]]></file>
    </pre_existing_files>

    <expected_files>
        <!-- exact content the agent must produce -->
        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/alerts/error_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
hour,occurrences
14,2
15,3
16,3
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/monitoring/alerts/latest_errors.log"><![CDATA[
2023-11-01 15:12:29 ERROR Timeout while reading from socket
2023-11-01 15:30:45 ERROR Timeout while reading from socket
2023-11-01 16:01:02 ERROR Failed to send notification
2023-11-01 16:05:18 ERROR Failed to send notification
2023-11-01 16:10:59 ERROR Failed to send notification
]]></file>
    </expected_files>
